request.question: Cult of Personality is a song used by CM Punk as an entrance song for the WWE event held at which New Jersey stadium?

title: WrestleMania 29
Champion (Clement Marfo & The Frontline song)
Luke Gallows
WWE Slam City
King of the Ring (2010)
Elimination Chamber (2013)
Royal Rumble (2013)
Judgment of Princeton
Cult of Personality (song)
WrestleMania XXX
sentences: WrestleMania 29 (billed in print ads as WrestleMania NY/NJ) was the twenty-ninth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE, held on April 7, 2013 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.
Nine professional wrestling matches were contested at the event, with one being shown on the pre-show.
"Champion" is a single release by English group Clement Marfo & The Frontline.
The song was released in the United Kingdom as a digital download on July 20, 2012.
The song peaked to number 38 on the UK Singles Chart.
The song features on the soundtrack of the video game "F1 2011" and the 2010 film "StreetDance 3D", and was the official theme song of the WWE Royal Rumble pay-per-view in 2013.
The song was also used CM Punk's 434 day reign as WWE Champion as a tribute
Andrew William Hankinson (born December 22, 1983) is an American professional wrestler and promoter, currently signed with WWE on the Raw brand under the ring name Luke Gallows.

During his first stint with the promotion, Hankinson also appeared briefly as an imposter of the wrestler Kane during 2006 and then returned as Festus, a seemingly mentally handicapped character who teamed with Jesse (Ray Gordy), but the team achieved little success.

After disappearing from television, Hankinson later became the enforcer and "disciple" of CM Punk, sporting a cleaner, militant look, with the Festus character's "real identity" revealed as Luke Gallows.
His first run in WWE ended in 2010.

He is perhaps best known for working for New Japan Pro Wrestling under the ring name Doc Gallows, and he also wrestled prominently under the TNA banner, where he was a member of the heel Aces & Eights stable under the ring name D.O.C. (Director of Chaos).

Along with Karl Anderson, Hankinson has won the WWE Raw Tag Team Championship once since returning to WWE, and he also held the IWGP Tag Team Championship three times with Anderson, making them one of only three tag teams (with The Steiner Brothers and The Dudley Boyz) to have held both titles between those promotions.
WWE Slam City was an animated show produced by WWE, which aired on the WWE Network and Wednesday evenings on Nicktoons.
WWE Slam City was based on the Mattel action figure series starring toyetic versions of the WWE roster circa 2013.

The series, filmed in the next generation of stop-motion animation, features a new WWE animated character The Finisher, who fires all of the WWE wrestlers and sends them to Slam City to find day jobs.
The wrestlers are plunged into new career challenges as they pack every street corner with work to do and scores to settle.

With John Cena as an auto mechanic, Rey Mysterio as a traffic guard, Alberto Del Rio as a coffee house barista, Mark Henry as a pizza shop mascot, CM Punk as an ice-cream man, Dolph Ziggler as an industry leader, Randy Orton as a zookeeper, Kane as a chef and Sheamus as a theater usher.
Daniel Bryan, The Miz, Big Show, Brock Lesnar, Santino Marella and Damien Sandow are still jobless but Santino and Sandow are the most desperate to find one.
WWE Legends Stone Cold and The Rock are also featured in Slam City.
The 'Stretch Move' feature of Slam City characters is highlighted in WWE Slam City.
The 2010 King of the Ring was a special edition of "Raw".
that aired on November 29, 2010, at the Wells Fargo Center in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.
The program featured the nineteenth King of the Ring tournament and first since 2008.
Sheamus defeated John Morrison in the finals of the tournament to become King of the Ring.

Unlike the previous tournament that featured wrestlers from the "Raw," "SmackDown" and "ECW" brands, the 2010 version of the tournament only featured wrestlers from "Raw" and "SmackDown" (following the closure of the ECW brand in February 2010).

The last King of the Ring tournament which only featured wrestlers from both "Raw" and "SmackDown" was held in 2002 (following the WWE Brand Extension the previous month, but before the ECW brand was introduced as a third brand in 2006).

Despite the event being mainly focused on the King of the Ring concept, the main event featured Jerry Lawler (who was celebrating his birthday that day) facing the new WWE Champion The Miz for the WWE Championship in a Tables, Ladders and Chairs match which marked Lawler's first WWE Championship match.
Lawler lost due to distractions from Alex Riley and Michael Cole.
CM Punk was joined in commentary on the show.
Elimination Chamber (2013) (also known as No Escape (2013) in Germany) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on February 17, 2013, at the New Orleans Arena in New Orleans, Louisiana.
The pay-per-view was the fourth annual Elimination Chamber event.
This year's event was headlined by The Rock defending the WWE Championship against CM Punk.
Royal Rumble (2013) was the twenty-sixth annual Royal Rumble professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on January 27, 2013 at the US Airways Center in Phoenix, Arizona.
It was the first WWE pay-per-view of 2013, and featured John Cena winning the traditional Royal Rumble match.
In the main event, The Rock defeated CM Punk for the WWE Championship, recapturing the title for the first time since 2002, and ending Punk's long reign at 434 days.

The Judgment of Princeton was a wine tasting (or blind tasting) event held on 8 June 2012 during a conference of the American Association of Wine Economists held at Princeton University in Princeton, New Jersey.

The purpose of this event was to compare, by a blind tasting, of several French wines against wines produced in New Jersey in order to gauge the quality and development of the New Jersey wine industry.
Because New Jersey's wine industry is relatively young and small, it has received little attention in the world wine market.

The state's wine production has experienced growth in recent years largely as a result of state legislators offering new opportunities for winery licensing and repealing Prohibition-era laws that have constrained the industry's development in past years.

This event was modeled after a 1976 blind tasting event dubbed the "Judgment of Paris" in which French wines were compared to several wines produced in California when that state's wine industry was similarly young and developing.
The New Jersey wine industry heralded the results and asserted that the rating of New Jersey wines by the blind tasting's judges was a victory for the state's wine industry.
"Cult of Personality" is a song by rock band Living Colour.
It was their second single off their debut album, "Vivid", released on July 14, 1988.
"Cult of Personality" reached No. 13 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and No. 9 on the "Billboard" Album Rock Tracks chart.
It also won the Grammy Award for Best Hard Rock Performance in 1990.
Its music video earned the MTV Video Music Award for Best Group Video and MTV Video Music Award for Best New Artist.
The song was ranked No. 69 on VH1's "100 Greatest Hard Rock Songs".
The solo was ranked No. 87 in "Guitar World"' s "100 Greatest Guitar Solos" list.
It was also selected for inclusion in the musical reference book, "1001 Songs You Must Hear Before You Die: And 10,001 You Must Download".
The sections before the bridge are similar to a hook in Black Sabbath's "Wheels Of Confusion".
In 2007, the song was re-recorded and released for the video game "".
The re-recording later appeared in "Guitar Hero Smash Hits".
It also appeared in the video game "" on the radio station "Radio X".
The song is also the entrance music for former ROH and WWE wrestler turned UFC fighter, CM Punk, and Living Colour performed the song live for his entrance at WrestleMania 29.
The song also appeared on the soundtrack for the 2015 video game "NBA 2K16".
WrestleMania XXX (also written as WrestleMania 30) was the thirtieth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on April 6, 2014, at the Mercedes-Benz Superdome in New Orleans, Louisiana.
It was the first WWE event simultaneously broadcast live on pay-per-view television and WWE's new streaming media service, the WWE Network.
Seven professional wrestling matches were scheduled on the event's card and one pre-show match was streamed on the WWE Network.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here. Emit exactly one field named `summary`.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A one-line header naming the primary topic(s) identified in the passages.
- 1–4 short lines enumerating concrete facts directly supported by the passages. Extract entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Do not add or infer facts not present in the passages.
- An explicit statement that names the missing bridge fact or relation needed for the next hop (express in abstract role terms, e.g., "missing bridge fact: [relation] between [candidate entity] and [target attribute]"). If passages supply candidate bridge entities but do not confirm the required relation, label them as "possible bridge entity" and state what is unverified for each.
- 1–4 prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (highest priority first). Each directive must be concrete and section-aware: specific page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Early life", "History"), or tight search queries/keywords. Prefer section-level targets and tight searches over broad suggestions.

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` compact: one-line header plus a total of 1–4 brief lines (sentences or bullet lines) covering the factual spans, the missing bridge fact, and the prioritized retrieval directives.
- Preserve exact factual spans from the passages; when quoting, keep quotes very short (<= ~15 words) and only if verbatim in the passages.
- Do not assert a final answer or resolve facts here. If the passages do fully and unambiguously contain the required relation, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but still do not produce a final answer.
- If multiple candidate entities or variant names appear, enumerate the top 1–2 candidates and mark the name as ambiguous, stating what is unverified for each.
- Do not invent facts, hypothesize, or add extraneous context not grounded in the provided passages.

Output expectation: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary as specified above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

When composing the query, select the minimal anchor token(s) visible in `question` and/or `summary_1` that point to the missing page or fact. You may append a single short attribute keyword (for example country member occupations record season) only when that keyword is needed to disambiguate the evidence type. Do not introduce any other new tokens.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Use these operational rules when choosing a template:
- If summary_1 explicitly lists two or more distinct page anchors as next-hop targets, combine those visible anchor tokens into one compact multi-anchor query and append a short attribute keyword naming the evidence type (for example country member occupations record).
- If summary_1 flags a single missing anchor or a missing_titles list is present, emit a single-anchor metadata-style query that places the missing anchor token first and appends a concise attribute keyword naming the desired fact.
- When summary_1 names multiple candidate entities and the missing fact concerns one candidate’s membership, order, or relation to a canonical collection, prefer a connector-style query that pairs the highest-priority candidate token (first or prioritized in summary_1) with the canonical collection token plus a short attribute keyword requesting membership or order.
- When individual member tokens are present but the needed retrieval is about that individual, prefer member-token + group-anchor + member (or equivalent short attribute) to target the individual’s page rather than issuing a broad group-level query.

Priority rules for ambiguity and disambiguation:
- If summary_1 lists multiple candidate anchors or otherwise signals ambiguity, emit a single connector-style query that concatenates the prioritized anchor tokens (use the order or priority visible in summary_1) and then append exactly one short attribute token or a visible year token for disambiguation.
- If summary_1 contains a comma-separated list of individuals and the missing evidence concerns a member, prefer the earliest-listed individual token paired with the visible group token and append the single attribute token member.
- If the question contains a visible year token that disambiguates a title, include that year token before the attribute token in the query.
- If none of the above applies, emit a single-anchor query using the most relevant visible token and at most one short attribute token when the question implies an attribute.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Additional constraints (must follow):
- The first sentence must be an answer-first concise identification (a direct answer or concise identification). If the `context` already contains such a sentence, preserve it exactly unless a passage explicitly and directly contradicts it; otherwise derive that first sentence from the clearest passage.
- After the first sentence, append at most one short supporting sentence for each additional relevant named entity only when the passages supply corroborating attributes. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the inputs (prefer the form used in the context when there is a name-variant conflict) and paraphrase no more than one or two simple attributes.
- When a queried binary/status attribute for an entity is not stated in the provided passages, indicate that absence concisely within that entity's supporting sentence (for example, with a short parenthetical or clause saying the status is not given in the provided evidence).
- If the passages explicitly assert a relationship between two or more entities that is directly relevant to the question, include one short relationship sentence after the per-entity supporting sentence(s) describing that relationship; do not embed the same relationship claim redundantly in multiple sentences.
- If relevant entities exceed the available supporting-sentence budget under the 1–3 sentence limit, prioritize entities in this order: entities explicitly named in the question, entities named in the preserved first sentence, then other context entities. Do not exceed the 1–3 sentence cap.

Follow these additional concise operational rules when integrating evidence:
- Prefer the context-preferred name form for the preserved first (answer) sentence; when choosing a canonical name for supporting sentences, prefer the context form if there is a name-variant conflict across inputs.
- If a passage explicitly contradicts a preserved context sentence, replace the preserved sentence with a passage-supported first sentence or note the contradiction concisely in that first sentence.
- For each supporting sentence, start with the chosen canonical name and include only attributes explicitly present in the provided passages; if the passages do not assert a requested status or attribute, state that absence briefly rather than inferring the attribute.
- Do not add fields, requests for more retrieval, or change the input/output interface. Keep the `summary` to 1–3 sentences exactly and avoid extra commentary or formatting.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add any new facts or external information.
- First run a mandatory binary detection: determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Do not infer that the final state is unresolved merely because `summary_1` uses earlier unresolved or bridge language; prefer `summary_2` when it clarifies `summary_1`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

Binary detection must run first. Only if the question is not a binary yes/no or the binary detector does not apply, proceed to extraction.

Conservative extractive tie-breaking (apply only when binary detection does not produce a single-token answer):
- When a contiguous longer verbatim phrase in either summary strictly contains a shorter candidate (adds adjacent words that are part of the name or label), emit the longest contiguous verbatim phrase exactly as it appears in the summaries.
- For personal names where one verbatim form is a substring of another, prefer the longest verbatim personal-name span found in either summary; if multiple distinct spans tie for length, prefer the form from `summary_2`.
- If either summary presents an alternate token parenthetically immediately adjacent to a name/place (e.g., canonical short form in parentheses), emit that parenthetical token exactly as shown. If both summaries present parenthetical tokens, prefer the one in `summary_2`.
- If the summaries do not explicitly state the requested relation but exactly one candidate entity of the relevant role appears verbatim across the summaries and no summary contradicts the attribution, emit that single verbatim entity. If multiple distinct candidates appear or there is any contradiction, do not apply this rule and return the unresolved outcome.
- Prefer wording from `summary_2` only as a tie-breaker, except when the longest-contiguous-span or parenthetical-token rules require choosing a longer or parenthetical form present in `summary_1`.

Extraction and formatting rules (apply only when binary detection does not produce a single-token answer):
- Person-name selection: if a longer verbatim personal-name string appears in either summary and contains a shorter candidate as a substring, emit the longest contiguous verbatim name exactly as it appears in the summaries. Prefer wording from `summary_2` when both summaries provide the same evidence but differ in form.
- Denomination/type extraction: for questions asking a "kind", "type", or "denomination" of a role, extract and emit exactly the minimal token or short phrase that appears immediately adjacent to the role word in the summaries (the word/phrase answering "what kind"). If multiple adjacent phrases appear, prefer the longest contiguous phrase unless that creates contradiction.
- Event-name extraction: when summaries show a contiguous proper-name followed by a generic event-type noun, emit only the contiguous proper-name core. Strip any leading articles and remove the trailing generic event-type noun; emit only the proper-name portion exactly as given in the summaries.
- Nationality handling: if both summaries unambiguously state the same nationality phrase and at least one summary places the literal single word "citizen" immediately after that nationality phrase, append exactly one space and the single word "citizen" to the nationality phrase. Otherwise emit the nationality phrase exactly as given in the summaries.

General formatting constraints for all outputs:
- Always emit the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries and do not add explanation, context, punctuation, or extra words.
- Do not introduce external facts, additional descriptors, dates, roles, or explanatory text not present in the summaries.
- Do not normalize or invent name variants that are not verbatim in the summaries.
- If the summaries conflict, lack the required adjacent phrasing, or do not present a clear verbatim form for the requested attribute, do not guess—use the module's normal unresolved output.

Output contract:
- Produce a single field `answer` whose contents follow the rules above. The `answer` field must contain only the minimal string supported by `summary_1` and `summary_2` and no additional tokens, punctuation, or explanation.

Use only the provided summaries as evidence and follow the conservative tie-breaking rules above when extracting the final minimal verbatim span.

hop1_docs: Cult of Personality (song) | "Cult of Personality" is a song by rock band Living Colour.  It was their second single off their debut album, "Vivid", released on July 14, 1988.  "Cult of Personality" reached No. 13 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and No. 9 on the "Billboard" Album Rock Tracks chart.  It also won the Grammy Award for Best Hard Rock Performance in 1990.  Its music video earned the MTV Video Music Award for Best Group Video and MTV Video Music Award for Best New Artist.  The song was ranked No. 69 on VH1's "100 Greatest Hard Rock Songs".  The solo was ranked No. 87 in "Guitar World"' s "100 Greatest Guitar Solos" list.  It was also selected for inclusion in the musical reference book, "1001 Songs You Must Hear Before You Die: And 10,001 You Must Download".  The sections before the bridge are similar to a hook in Black Sabbath's "Wheels Of Confusion".  In 2007, the song was re-recorded and released for the video game "".  The re-recording later appeared in "Guitar Hero Smash Hits".  It also appeared in the video game "" on the radio station "Radio X".  The song is also the entrance music for former ROH and WWE wrestler turned UFC fighter, CM Punk, and Living Colour performed the song live for his entrance at WrestleMania 29.  The song also appeared on the soundtrack for the 2015 video game "NBA 2K16".

WWE: Uncaged | WWE: Uncaged is a compilation album of unreleased professional wrestling entrance theme songs which was released by WWE on December 16, 2016 on online music stores.  The album features multiple tracks that were not available to the general public before the release of the album, most notably the first version of Chris Jericho's theme song "Break the Walls Down", which was composed by Jim Johnston and performed by Adam Morenoff used for his WWE debut back in 1999.  The album also features the acoustic versions of Batista's theme song, "I Walk Alone" and Randy Orton's theme song, "Voices" which were used for their WrestleMania 30 video packages, which aired on WWE programming before WrestleMania to get fans hyped and excited for their matches at the event.

Champion (Clement Marfo & The Frontline song) | "Champion" is a single release by English group Clement Marfo & The Frontline.  The song was released in the United Kingdom as a digital download on July 20, 2012.  The song peaked to number 38 on the UK Singles Chart.  The song features on the soundtrack of the video game "F1 2011" and the 2010 film "StreetDance 3D", and was the official theme song of the WWE Royal Rumble pay-per-view in 2013.  The song was also used CM Punk's 434 day reign as WWE Champion as a tribute

WWE No Mercy | No Mercy is an annual professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.  The first No Mercy was held on May 16, 1999 in Manchester, England.  The event was held annually in October from 1999 to 2008, and from 2003 to 2006 it was exclusive to the SmackDown!  brand.  In 2016, the event returned as a "SmackDown" exclusive following the reinstated brand extension and aired on the WWE Network for the first time.  However in 2017, the pay-per-view will be a Raw branded event.  The song "No Mercy" by Jim Johnston was used as a regular theme song for the events in 2002, 2004, 2006, and 2007.  In the 2016 and 2017 events, the theme song was "No Mercy" by KIT.

Line in the Sand (Motörhead song) | Line in the Sand is a 2004 rock song from WWE's soundtrack album, "".  The song used to be the entrance theme of the professional wrestling alliance, Evolution.  The song is performed by Motörhead.

WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour | The WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour was a professional wrestling televised event held by World Wrestling Entertainment and which took place at the Westpac Stadium in Wellington, New Zealand on March 4, 2006.  The event was taped and later aired on WWE 24/7 in the United States.

Stadium anthem | Stadium anthems or sports anthems or arena anthems are a musical genre identifying songs that are played over the public address systems at stadiums and arenas during breaks in the action to rally the fans.  Unlike college fight songs, most stadium anthems were not written primarily for use at sports events, though compilations such as "ESPN Presents Stadium Anthems" and the ""Jock"" series occasionally feature remixed versions of these songs designed to segue together or to accentuate the rhythm or other elements of the songs.  Some football events have their own anthems, but they are not played during breaks, but they are played as entrance music.  The most notable anthems are the FIFA Anthem and the UEFA Champions League Anthem.

response.summary_1:
Topic: "Cult of Personality" (Living Colour) — CM Punk — WrestleMania 29
- Facts from passages: "Cult of Personality" is a song by Living Colour (released July 14, 1988) and is the "entrance music" for CM Punk; "Living Colour performed the song live for his entrance at WrestleMania 29."
- Missing bridge fact: which New Jersey stadium hosted WrestleMania 29 (possible bridge entity: WrestleMania 29 is named in passages but the stadium/venue/location is unverified).
- Next-hop retrievals (priority order): 1) WrestleMania 29 — Wikipedia lede/infobox (venue/location) and "Event"/"Background" sections; 2) CM Punk — Wikipedia "Entrance themes" or any "WrestleMania" / "Career" sections referencing WrestleMania 29; 3) Tight search/query: "WrestleMania 29 stadium New Jersey MetLife Stadium East Rutherford".
hop2_docs: WrestleMania XXVI | WrestleMania XXVI was the twenty-sixth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE), which took place on March 28, 2010, at the University of Phoenix Stadium in the Phoenix suburb of Glendale, Arizona.  This was the first WrestleMania since WrestleMania XI with a non-title match as a main event, the first WrestleMania to be held in the state of Arizona and the third to be held in an open-air venue, after WrestleMania IX and WrestleMania XXIV.

WrestleMania X8 | WrestleMania X8 was the eighteenth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) produced by the World Wrestling Federation (WWF).  It took place on March 17, 2002, at the SkyDome in Toronto, Canada, the second WrestleMania at that venue (following WrestleMania VI in 1990).  The event marked the final WrestleMania event under the WWF name.  The record-breaking attendance for the SkyDome of 68,237 grossed approximately $6.1 million CAD ($3.9 million USD).  WrestleMania weekend also included WWF Fan Axxess at the Canadian National Exhibition's Automotive Building.

WrestleMania 2 | WrestleMania 2 (sequentially known as WrestleMania II) was the second annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by the World Wrestling Federation (WWF) (although the first WrestleMania was only on pay-per-view in select areas).  It took place on Monday, April 7, 1986, making it the only WrestleMania that was not held on the customary Sunday.  WrestleMania 2 took place at three venues: the Nassau Veterans Memorial Coliseum in Uniondale, New York; the Rosemont Horizon in Rosemont, Illinois; and the Los Angeles Memorial Sports Arena in Los Angeles, California.

WrestleMania 29 | WrestleMania 29 (billed in print ads as WrestleMania NY/NJ) was the twenty-ninth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE, held on April 7, 2013 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.  Nine professional wrestling matches were contested at the event, with one being shown on the pre-show.

WrestleMania 31 | WrestleMania 31 was the thirty-first annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.  It took place on March 29, 2015, at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, California.

WrestleMania XIV | WrestleMania XIV was the fourteenth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by the World Wrestling Federation (WWF), which was presented by M&M's and took place on March 29, 1998, at the FleetCenter in Boston, Massachusetts.

The Streak (wrestling) | The Streak was a series of 21 straight victories for professional wrestler The Undertaker (Mark Calaway) at WWE's premier annual event, WrestleMania.  It began at WrestleMania VII in 1991 when he beat Jimmy Snuka, with the final win coming against CM Punk at WrestleMania 29 in 2013; The Undertaker was absent from WrestleMania X (1994) and WrestleMania 2000, owing to injury.  Overall, he defeated 18 men during The Streak, which included three bouts with Triple H and two each opposite Kane and Shawn Michaels, as well as a handicap match against A-Train and Big Show at WrestleMania XIX.  Although part of professional wrestling storyline, this run of victories was described by sports news publication "The 42" as the "greatest winning streak in sport", while Sky Sports likened it to that of boxer Floyd Mayweather.
response.summary_2: WrestleMania 29 was held at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey. Living Colour performed "Cult of Personality" live for CM Punk's entrance at that event.
